Boundary

The boundary of this geometry. A polygon's boundary is a polyline. A polyline's boundary is a multipoint. A point or multipoint's boundary is an empty point or multipoint.

virtual IGeometry Boundary { get; }

Returns

IGeometry

ClipToDomain()

Clips the geometry to the domain of the spatial reference. Useful for ensuring that buffers can be fit within the spatial domain of the feature class to which they are being added.

virtual void ClipToDomain()

Close()

Closes all rings in this polygon. The resulting polygon may not be simple.

Sample Snippet

ConnectedComponentBag

Creates and returns a geometry bag containing polygons that represent connected components of the original. The rings of the output polygons are references to rings in the original.

virtual IGeometryBag ConnectedComponentBag { get; }

Returns

IGeometryBag

DensifyByAngle(double, double)

Converts this polycurve into a piecewise linear approximation of itself. Vertices are introduced at points where the included angle between tangents at those point is maxAngleDeviation.

<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes"><span style="mso-spacerun: yes">
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>

<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes">&nbsp;</span></p>

<span style="COLOR: teal">Random</span> random = <span style=

"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
<span style=

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>
<span style="COLOR: blue">double</span> x = Min + (Max - Min) *
random.NextDouble();</span></p>
<p class="MsoNormal" style=
"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes"><span style="mso-spacerun: yes">
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>
<span style="COLOR: blue">double</span> y = Min + (Max - Min) *
random.NextDouble();</span></p>
<p class="MsoNormal" style=
"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes"><span style="mso-spacerun: yes">
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>
<span style="COLOR: blue">double</span> z = Min + (Max - Min) *
random.NextDouble();</span></p>
<p class="MsoNormal" style=
"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes">&nbsp;</span></p>

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>
<span style="COLOR: blue">return</span> ConstructPoint3D(x, y,
z);</span></p>
<p class="MsoNormal" style=
"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes"><span style="mso-spacerun: yes">
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span> }</span></p>

"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: black; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes; mso-fareast-font-family: 'Times New Roman'">
z)</span></p>

<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: black; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes; mso-fareast-font-family: 'Times New Roman'">

{</span></p>

Parameters

other IGeometry

Returns

bool

ExteriorRingBag

Creates and returns a geometry bag that contains references to all exterior rings.

virtual IGeometryBag ExteriorRingBag { get; }

Returns

IGeometryBag

Examples

Sample Snippet

ExteriorRingCount

The number of exterior rings.

virtual int ExteriorRingCount { get; }

Returns

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

ExtrapolateMs(esriExtrapolationEnum, int, int, int, int)

Extrapolates the Ms at one or both ends of the geometry based on the M interval between the fromIndex and the toIndex.

virtual void ExtrapolateMs(esriExtrapolationEnum extrapolationStyle, int fromPart, int FromPoint, int toPart, int ToPoint)

Parameters

extrapolationStyle esriExtrapolationEnum
fromPart int
FromPoint int
toPart int
ToPoint int

FindExteriorRing(IRing) 

virtual IRing FindExteriorRing(IRing interiorRing)

Parameters

interiorRing IRing

Returns

IRing

FromPoint

The 'from' point of the curve.

virtual IPoint FromPoint { get; set; }

Returns

IPoint

Examples

Sample Snippet

Generalize(double)

Generalizes this polycurve using the Douglas-Peucker algorithm.

virtual void Generalize(double maxAllowableOffset)

Parameters

maxAllowableOffset double

Examples

Sample Snippet

Generalize3D(double)

Generalizes this polycurve using the Douglas-Peucker algorithm.

virtual void Generalize3D(double maxAllowableOffset)

Parameters

maxAllowableOffset double

Examples

Sample Snippet

GeoNormalize()

Shifts longitudes, if need be, into a continuous range of 360 degrees.

virtual void GeoNormalize()

Examples

Sample Snippet

GeoNormalizeEx(ISpatialReference, double) 

virtual void GeoNormalizeEx(ISpatialReference SR, double denseDistance)

Parameters

SR ISpatialReference
denseDistance double

GeoNormalizeFromLongitude(double)

Normalizes longitudes into a continuous range containing the longitude. This method is obsolete.

virtual void GeoNormalizeFromLongitude(double Longitude)

Parameters

Longitude double

Examples

Sample Snippet

GeodesicDensify(double) 

virtual void GeodesicDensify(double maxSegmentLength)

Parameters

maxSegmentLength double

GeometriesChanged()

Tells this geometry collection that some of its geometries have been altered. Use this method on polylines, polygons and multipatches after directly editing one of its parts.

virtual void GeometriesChanged()

Geometry[int]

A reference to the ith geometry.

virtual IGeometry Geometry[int Index] { get; }

Index int

Returns

IGeometry

GetClassID(out Guid) 

virtual void GetClassID(out Guid pClassID)

Parameters

pClassID Guid

GetConnectedComponents(int, out IPolygon)

Populates an array with polygons that represent connected components of the original. The rings of the output polygons are references to rings in the original.

virtual void GetConnectedComponents(int numComponentsRequested, out IPolygon components)

Parameters

numComponentsRequested int
components IPolygon

Examples

Sample Snippet

GetConnectedComponents2(out IPolygon[])

Populates an array with polygons that represent connected components of the original. The rings of the output polygons are references to rings in the original.

virtual void GetConnectedComponents2(out IPolygon[] components)

Parameters

components IPolygon[]

Examples

Sample Snippet

GetDistancesAtM(bool, double)

Returns an array of distances along the polyline at which is located the specified M. If the geometry's M's are monotonic along the geometry then the array will only have one element.

virtual object GetDistancesAtM(bool asRatio, double M)

Parameters

asRatio bool
M double

Returns

object

Examples

Sample Snippet

GetMsAtDistance(double, bool)

Returns M values at the distance along the geometry. An array of one or two Ms is returned. Two Ms can be returned if the given distance is exactly at the beginning or ending of a part.

virtual object GetMsAtDistance(double distance, bool asRatio)

Parameters

distance double
asRatio bool

Returns

object

Examples

Sample Snippet

GetOutermostComponents(int, out int, out IPolygon, out bool)

Populates an array with polygons that represent outermost components of the original and all components within (islands within lakes). The rings of the output polygons are references to rings in the original.

virtual void GetOutermostComponents(int numComponentsRequested, out int numComponentsReturned, out IPolygon components, out bool moreComponentsExist)

Parameters

numComponentsRequested int
numComponentsReturned int
components IPolygon
moreComponentsExist bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

GetOutermostComponents2(out IPolygon[], out bool)

Populates an array with polygons that represent outermost components of the original and all components within (islands within lakes). The rings of the output polygons are references to rings in the original.

virtual void GetOutermostComponents2(out IPolygon[] components, out bool moreComponentsExist)

Parameters

components IPolygon[]
moreComponentsExist bool

Index int
Count int
Points IPoint

"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes"><span style="mso-spacerun: yes">
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>
<span style="COLOR: teal">Random</span> random = <span style=
"COLOR: blue">new</span> <span style=
"COLOR: teal">Random</span>();</span></p>
<p class="MsoNormal" style=
"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
<span style=

&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>
<span style="COLOR: blue">double</span> x = Min + (Max - Min) *
random.NextDouble();</span></p>
<p class="MsoNormal" style=
"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes"><span style="mso-spacerun: yes">
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>

random.NextDouble();</span></p>
<p class="MsoNormal" style=
"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
<span style=
"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes"><span style="mso-spacerun: yes">
&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;</span>
<span style="COLOR: blue">double</span> z = Min + (Max - Min) *
random.NextDouble();</span></p>
<p class="MsoNormal" style=
"MARGIN: 0in 0in 0pt; LINE-HEIGHT: normal; mso-layout-grid-align: none">
&nbsp;</p>

"FONT-SIZE: 10pt; COLOR: black; FONT-FAMILY: 'Courier New'; mso-no-proof: yes; mso-fareast-font-family: 'Times New Roman'">

PointCount

The number of points in the collection.

virtual int PointCount { get; }

Returns

int

Examples

Sample Snippet

PointIDAware

Indicates whether or not the geometry is aware of and capable of handling PointIDs.

virtual bool PointIDAware { get; set; }

Returns

bool

Examples

Sample Snippet

PointIDSimple

Indicates if all PointID values for this geometry are well-defined. Only works if geometry is aware of PointIDs.

virtual bool PointIDSimple { get; }

Returns

bool

QueryExteriorRings(ref IRing)

Populates an array with references to all exterior rings. This method does not marshall correctly out-of-proc. Use QueryExteriorRingsEx on the IPolygon2 interface instead.

virtual void QueryExteriorRings(ref IRing exteriorRings)

Parameters

exteriorRings IRing

Examples

Sample Snippet

QueryExteriorRingsEx(int, out IRing)

Returns an array of references to exterior rings, up to a maximum of the number requested.

numExteriorRingsRequested int
exteriorRings IRing

QuerySegments(int, int, out ISegment)

Returns references to some of the input segments. This method is intended for internal use only.

SimplifyEx(bool, bool, bool)

Same as ITopologicalOperator::Simplify, but with some additional options that affect performance and how the interior is classified.

Parameters

preserveFromToPoints bool

SimplifySpaghetti()

Simplifies a polygon but doesn't assume that rings need to be explicitly closed.

Converts this curve into a smooth curve containing only Bezier curve segments.

Parameters

maxAllowableOffset double

Sample Snippet

Snap()

Snaps ms to the precision of the spatial reference associated with the geometry. M snapping does not happen automatically as part of calling IGeometry::SnapToSpatialReference.

virtual void Snap()

Examples

Sample Snippet

SnapToSpatialReference()

Moves points of this geometry so that they can be represented in the precision of the geometry's associated spatial reference system.
